task_completion.description: The Multiplanar Reconstruction (MPR) viewer is open and displaying the loaded multi-slice CT series.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An active medical imaging viewer window (MPR mode) is visible on the screen, showing CT scan slices.

task_completion.description: Maximum Intensity Projection (MIP) mode is enabled in the viewer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A UI toggle, dropdown, or button indicating 'MIP' or 'Maximum Intensity Projection' is selected or highlighted.

task_completion.description: The slab thickness is set to at least 15mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A thickness input field, slider, or overlay text in the viewer UI displays a value of '15' (or greater) mm.

task_completion.description: The viewer displays a continuous view of the diagonal vessel, confirming the thick-slab MIP is active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main image viewport shows a solid, continuous bright diagonal vessel (characteristic of a thick-slab MIP), rather than a small dot or broken line (which would indicate a thin slice).

task_completion.description: The agent initiates the export of the current MIP view as a PNG image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As', 'Export', or 'Capture' dialog box is open, with the file type set to PNG.

task_completion.description: The image is exported to the exact requested path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog or terminal confirmation shows the file being saved exactly to '~/DICOM/exports/mip_projection.png' or '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/mip_projection.png'.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual MPR viewer GUI to generate the MIP projection.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks, menu navigation, and UI interaction within the medical viewer, rather than terminal commands (like ImageMagick or Python scripts) bypassing the UI.

integrity.description: The agent exported the image from the viewer rather than copying or renaming an existing file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The export dialog is seen originating from the MPR viewer application itself.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected output path into a text editor to spoof completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: The path '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/mip_projection.png' is entered into a legitimate file manager or save dialog, not a text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected export path is exactly '/home/ga/DICOM/exports/mip_projection.png'. A successful 15mm thick-slab MIP will visually show a continuous diagonal vessel. In the underlying data, a thin slice has <1200 bright pixels (typically ~901), appearing broken or dot-like. A proper 15mm MIP accumulates >1500 bright pixels (typically ~3185), making the vessel appear continuous and bright.
